headline: Morgan Stanley raises Intel target but prefers these chip stocks more
summary: Investing.com -- Morgan Stanley raised its price target on Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC) to $56 from $41, citing stronger server demand and higher expected earnings, but said memory chipmakers Micron and SanDisk offer better risk-reward for investors looking to play the AI-driven surge in processor demand.

headline: Latest News In AI Chips - Alchip Advances AI Processor Design With 3DIC Platform

This platform utilizes flexible chiplet architectures and advanced packaging integration to address system-level complexities in AI infrastructure. headline: Micron’s Sold Out HBM Capacity Tests Valuation And AI Cash Flow Story
summary: Micron Technology’s advanced memory and high bandwidth memory (HBM) supply is reported to be fully committed through 2026 and into 2027. The company is securing long term capacity agreements with major AI data center customers, tying up HBM and DRAM output well beyond the typical cycle. This extends visibility into AI infrastructure demand and highlights Micron’s role as a key memory supplier to leading chipmakers. For investors tracking NasdaqGS:MU, this news comes as the stock trades...
